# SRC001 - How Advertising Works: What Do We Really Know?

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | Vakratsas & Ambler |
| Year | 1999 |
| Source type | Peer-reviewed review |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/002224299906300103) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic synthesis; older but foundational.

## Core takeaway

Advertising does not operate through one clean hierarchy. Cognition, affect, experience, memory, and behavior interact.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use this to stop the agent from treating ads as direct command-to-purchase. Require ads to route buyers into evidence and experience.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC001
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
